content:
How to Apply to Regenesys Business School

Thinking about studying at Regenesys Business School? Based in Johannesburg (Sandton), Gauteng, Regenesys is a Private Higher Education Institution (Business School) established in 1998. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: Regenesys Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameRegenesys Business School
TypePrivate Higher Education Institution (Business School)
LocationSandton, Johannesburg; Online delivery
Year Founded1998
Application Portalhttps://www.regenesys.net/apply
Application FeeR500
Applications OpenYear-round
Applications CloseRolling admissions
Minimum APS26 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone011 669 5000
Contact Emailadmissions@regenesys.net

Why Choose Regenesys?

Africa's most internationalised private business school.

  • Has students in over 160 countries, making it one of Africa's most international business schools
  • Offers South Africa's most affordable MBA programme
  • Strong focus on public sector and governance leadership programmes
  • Offers blended learning, combining online with face-to-face contact sessions

Faculties and Programmes at Regenesys

Regenesys offers qualifications across 5 faculties:

  • Business Administration
  • Finance
  • Governance
  • Management
  • Public Management

Popular programmes include: MBA, Postgraduate Diploma in Business Administration, BCom Business Administration, MPhil in Leadership.

APS Requirements for Regenesys

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at Regenesys is 26 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 26+ for undergraduate; postgraduate requires completed degree

Regenesys accepts a minimum APS of 26 for undergraduates. For the MBA and postgraduate programmes, a Bachelor's degree is required. Work experience is considered for MBA applicants without strong undergraduate results.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to Regenesys

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.regenesys.net. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the Regenesys online application portal at https://www.regenesys.net/apply. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R500.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in Year-round and close on Rolling admissions. Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from Regenesys, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for Regenesys

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: Year-round
  • Application Deadline: Rolling admissions
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at Regenesys

Studying at Regenesys can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • No NSFAS
  • Regenesys Financial Aid
  • Government departmental bursaries for public management
  • Employer-sponsored tuition

Note: As a private institution, Regenesys does not qualify for NSFAS funding. However, many private institutions offer their own bursaries, and bank student loans (Nedbank, FNB, Absa, Standard Bank) are widely available.

Preparing for Regenesys While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at Regenesys to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to Regenesys, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing Regenesys With Other Universities

Not sure if Regenesys is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorRegenesysTypical Alternative
TypePrivate Higher Education Institution (Business School)Traditional University / UoT
LocationJohannesburg (Sandton)Varies
Minimum APS2620–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR500R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleNoYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to Regenesys

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to Regenesys.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet Regenesys's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.
keywords: Regenesys Business School how to apply
quickAnswer: To apply to Regenesys Business School, visit https://www.regenesys.net/apply, pay the R500 application fee, and submit your documents before Rolling admissions. Minimum APS is 26 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to Regenesys?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at Regenesys is 26 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (26+ for undergraduate; postgraduate requires completed degree). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the Regenesys website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for Regenesys?
faqs.answer: Applications for Regenesys typically open in Year-round and close on Rolling admissions. We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.regenesys.net/apply to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for Regenesys?
faqs.answer: The application fee at Regenesys is R500. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the Regenesys website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does Regenesys accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: No, Regenesys is a private institution and does not qualify for NSFAS funding. However, the institution offers its own financial aid, and student loans from banks (Nedbank, FNB, Absa) are available. Some bursaries from private sector organisations may also apply.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to Regenesys?
faqs.answer: For your application to Regenesys, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
